pages.title: Meet Gareth Bacon MP
pages.meta_description: Latest news from Gareth Bacon, the Conservative Member of Parliament for Orpington.
headings: h2: Meet Gareth Bacon MP
h2: Watch Gareth Bacon MP's maiden speech
pages.text_excerpt: --> Meet Gareth Bacon MP top of page Home Meet Gareth Election 2024 News More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Meet Gareth Bacon MP My name is Gareth Bacon, and I am proud to be the Member of Parliament for Orpington. ​ I was born in Hong Kong but was raised, went to school and still live in Sidcup with my wife and daughter, close to the boundary of the Orpington constituency. ​ I took my Bachelors Degree at the University of Kent in 1996, before studying for a Masters Degree in European Politics at the same university the following year. The subject of my Master’s Degree dissertation was an examination of the democratic deficit in the European Union – I concluded that this was a problem that could not be resolved and that Britain would be better served by leaving the European Union. This forms much of the foundation for my support for Brexit. After university, I worked in financial services before moving into financial recruitment, latterly as a Director of a large financial recruitment business. ​ I was a London Assembly Member from 2008 until 2019, initially as a London-wide Member. I then represented the constituency of Bromley and Bexley, which includes Orpington, holding the Mayor to account and delivering for outer London. On the Assembly, I led the GLA Conservatives and worked tirelessly to protect our green spaces, keep our communities safe, and ensure that every penny of taxpayers' money is spent well. In the final 18 months of Boris Johnson's mayoralty, I served as Chairman of the London Fire & Emergency Planning Authority. This body oversaw the London Fire Brigade and co-ordinated the emergency planning work for London. In 1998, I was elected to serve as a local councillor in the London Borough of Bexley. For a total of nine years, I served as a Cabinet Member in a number of positions including as Deputy Leader of the Council, Cabinet Member for Finance and Corporate Services, and Cabinet Member for Environment and Public Realm. I was elected as the Member of Parliament for Orpington on 12 December 2019, and re-elected on 4 July 2024. I am proud to have been a strong voice for Orpington in Parliament and I have worked tirelessly to deliver for this beautiful part of historic Kent. Yours, Gareth Bacon Watch Gareth Bacon MP's maiden speech Follow me on social media © 2024 by Gareth Bacon Promoted by Mike Botting on behalf of Gareth Bacon both a 6 Sevenoaks Road, Orpington, Kent, BR6 9JJ Privacy Notice bottom of page

h2: Our green belt is under serious threat
pages.text_excerpt: --> News | Gareth Bacon top of page Home Meet Gareth Election 2024 News More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. All Posts Articles The "grey belt" con that's misleading the public Why can’t Ministers admit that the term “grey belt” is a dishonest concoction which they’ve created to mislead the pubic while they grab our precious grab green belt. garethbaconmp Apr 14 1 min read Under threat: Green Belt in St Paul's Cray There’s yet another threat to our precious green belt. This time it’s in St Paul’s Cray, off Chalkpit Avenue, where developers want to build up to 130 housing units on protected land. It will put even more pressure on our roads, schools, GP surgeries and local services — and it will desecrate our green space. Your voice matters. Please sign the petition to protect our green belt at St Paul’s Cray. Below, you can hear more from Conservative Council Candidate Dr Bola Adediran, garethbaconmp Mar 9 1 min read Middle East: We need clarity over plan to bring home British citizens I have been contacted by worried constituents whose relatives are trapped in Qatar. They have been asking for days what the government’s plan is to bring British citizens home. So far there’s been no clear plan, no confirmed repatriation flights, and no reassurance for families. This situation had been foreseeable for weeks as tensions escalated in the region. The government’s dithering and delay on every decision is an embarrassment to our country and it is failing our citiz garethbaconmp Mar 9 1 min read Parliamentary recognition for Phoenix Early Learners Very well deserved recognition in Parliament for the phenomenal team behind Phoenix Early Learners in Petts Wood. After the sudden closure of Crofton Early Learners, staff, carers, parents and volunteers rolled up their sleeves and - at great speed - built something new for local families. Very well done to all at Phoenix Early Learners. garethbaconmp Mar 4 1 min read Phoenix Early Leaners now open At the end of last year, Crofton Early Learners closed its doors. It was a huge blow for dedicated staff and local families suddenly left without childcare. But they didn’t give up. A determined group of parents, volunteers, and early-years professionals rolled up their sleeves and created Phoenix Early Learners. In just two months, they secured a venue, kitted it out, and got the green lights from Ofsted and the Charity Commission. It is now up and running and I was delig garethbaconmp Feb 20 1 min read Education on methanol poisoning In November 2024, Simone White from Orpington lost her life following a mass methanol poisoning incident. She was one of six tourists who died after drinking contaminated alcohol at a hostel in Laos. Simone’s family have since campaigned for increased awareness and education on the dangers of methanol poisoning abroad. This week, I submitted a petition to Parliament calling on the government to ensure that children are taught about the dangers of consuming bootleg alcohol garethbaconmp Feb 2 1 min read My objection to proposed green belt development in St Mary Cray I've now formally submitted my objection to the proposed St Mary Cray green belt development. The proposal to build around 350 homes on farmland south of Crockenhill Road is wrong — in principle and in practice. My objection covers the following: The land is not so called “grey belt”. It hasn’t been developed and doesn’t even meet the government’s own definition. Local roads are narrow and unsuitable for major construction traffic. GP surgeries, schools and local infrastr garethbaconmp Jan 19 1 min read Bromley paying the price for Sadiq Khan's housebuilding failure Housebuilding has fallen consistently in London over the last decade. The government’s response? To cut Sadiq Khan’s targets by 11% while jacking up targets for outer London authorities. I asked the Housing Secretary why people in Bromley should pay the price for the Mayor of London’s failure. garethbaconmp Jan 1

pages.title: Have your say on proposed green belt development | Gareth Bacon
pages.meta_description: Have your say on proposed green belt development in St Mary Cray.
headings: h2: PROPOSED GREEN BELT DEVELOPMENT IN ST MARY CRAY
pages.text_excerpt: --> Have your say on proposed green belt development | Gareth Bacon top of page Home Meet Gareth Election 2024 News More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. PROPOSED GREEN BELT DEVELOPMENT IN ST MARY CRAY Land Improvement Holdings have begun a publication exercise with local people, showcasing a housing development they would like to undertake in St Mary Cray. If they achieve planning permission, they aim to build approximately 350 units on farming land south of Crockenhill Road. To be clear, the site on which they wish to build is green belt land. A development of this nature will fundamentally change the look and feel of the area. Furthermore, if such a development is allowed to proceed, it may lead to more and more development in the green belt. Everyone is in agreement that we need more homes, but they must be built in the right places. Unfortunately, the government wants to make it easier to build on green belt land. But our green belt is precious, it is what makes Orpington such a beautiful and sustainable place to live, and if we allow it to be concreted over, it will be gone forever. No planning application has yet been submitted for this proposal, but one is expected in the near future, possibly as soon as Spring 2025. Please spare a few minutes to let me know your thoughts on this site by responding to the survey below. Thank you. Have your say Your First Name Your Last Name Your Email Your Address Do you think we should build homes on green belt land? Yes No Do you think we have the infrastructure to cope with hundreds of new homes in St Mary Cray? Yes No Do you believe that a development of approximately 350 units will change the look and feel of the area? Yes No Do you think we need to build more homes in St Mary Cray? Yes No If a planning application is submitted for this proposed development in St Mary Cray, should it be approved by the local authority? Yes No The government wants to reclassify parts of green belt land into so-called “grey belt” land so it is easier to build on. Should this be allowed to happen? Yes No Thinking of St Mary Cray and the wider Orpington constituency, where do you think new homes should be built? Is there anything else you would like to add regarding this proposed development or the green belt? I want to subscribe Gareth Bacon MP's newsletter. Submit Your answers have been submitted. Thank you for taking part. Follow me on social media © 2024 by Gareth Bacon Promoted by Mike Botting on behalf of Gareth Bacon both a 6 Sevenoaks Road, Orpington, Kent, BR6 9JJ Privacy Notice bottom of page
